hero.heading.start: Different people. Different ideas. Different tools.
hero.heading.end: One structure that defines the guardrails.
result.heading: What follows
result.1.title: Shared alignment
result.1.description: Different people, different ideas — aligned by a unified structure.
result.2.title: Seamless tool integration
result.2.description: Tools evolve, new ones are added — seamlessly integrable, because the framework makes it possible.
result.3.title: Frictionless change
result.3.description: Requirements change — change succeeds frictionlessly, the architecture is designed for it.
result.4.title: Lasting decisions
result.4.description: Teams evolve, technologies advance — decisions stay, anchored in the structure.
result.5.title: Compound improvements
result.5.description: Every improvement builds on the last — every investment is preserved and strengthens the next.
result.6.title: Transparent traceability
result.6.description: The standard is embedded in the structure — every decision is traceably documented.
principles.heading: Five principles
principles.1.title: Curiosity
principles.1.description: I approach every system without assumptions and examine what is actually there.
principles.2.title: Understanding
principles.2.description: I dig into the structures, dependencies, and connections behind it.
principles.3.title: Reflection
principles.3.description: I validate my findings from multiple directions until the full picture holds.
principles.4.title: Foresight
principles.4.description: I assess what the system needs to deliver in the future — and what it takes to get there.
principles.5.title: Essence
principles.5.description: I reduce to what truly matters.
services.heading: How this structure is built
services.diagnosis.title: Diagnosis
services.diagnosis.subtitle: Straight to the root cause
services.diagnosis.description: I trace your specific concern to its root cause — layer by layer through your system.
services.diagnosis.deliverable: A documented analysis — where the cause is, why, and what makes sense next.
services.design.title: Design
services.design.subtitle: Structure that works in practice
services.design.description: I design the target architecture — from the module structure to the rules that apply automatically.
services.design.deliverable: A complete architecture design — justified, documented, and immediately actionable.
services.advisory.title: Advisory
services.advisory.subtitle: Alignment that grows with you
services.advisory.description: A dedicated partner for architectural decisions — I support the architecture as your system evolves.
services.advisory.deliverable: Ongoing architectural guidance — reviews, feedback, and structural alignment.
services.handson.title: Hands-on architecture
services.handson.subtitle: Designed and delivered
services.handson.description: Architecture and implementation in one — directly in your codebase, anchored in the infrastructure.
casestudy.heading: Structure in action
casestudy.1.title: Performance questions — and what was behind them
casestudy.1.description: Scale-up, 8 developers, system getting slower. Diagnosis revealed: the cause lay in the structure. Three components with overlapping responsibilities — resolved through clear separation.
casestudy.1.result.label: Result
casestudy.1.result: The team had clarity about the cause and could act with precision.
casestudy.2.title: 15-year monolith — step-by-step migration
casestudy.2.description: Mid-size company, grown monolith. Diagnosis identified the structural leverage points. Playbook defined: what to decouple, in what order, with what guardrails.
casestudy.2.result.label: Result
casestudy.2.result: First independent service after 6 weeks. Deployment frequency tripled.
casestudy.3.title: New system — right from the start
casestudy.3.description: Startup, new product. No legacy, but the ambition to keep it that way. Playbook with structural guardrails so the system can grow cleanly.
casestudy.3.result.label: Result
casestudy.3.result: MVP after 10 weeks. Three teams working in parallel on the same system. Architecture convinced in the seed round.
process.heading: How it works
process.1.title: Initial conversation
process.1.description: We get to know each other and talk about your concern. I ask questions, you describe the situation.
process.2.title: Approach
process.2.description: Based on the conversation, we define the right approach — Diagnosis, Design, Advisory, or Hands-on. Each path has a clear goal and a concrete deliverable.
process.3.title: Scope
process.3.description: Together we define how deep the work goes and within which timeframe. The scope is set before work begins.
process.4.title: Execution
process.4.description: The actual work — analysis, design, guidance, or implementation. Results are clearly documented and traceable at any time.
process.5.title: Handover
process.5.description: The result is independently usable and clearly documented — ready to build on immediately.
about.heading: Who I am
about.1: Philipp Höllinger, software architect from Salzburg — with an obsession for the things that go unnoticed. The details that shape the future of a system.
about.2: My path went from developer to architect — in the automotive industry, the public sector, and software consulting. These domains taught me: when the structure is right, a system grows with every new team member, every new requirement, and every new tool.
about.3: I've designed and developed systems from the ground up, strengthened security architectures, and achieved measurable results through targeted redesign — including work for the Austrian government as part of an EU project.
about.4: What drives me: architectural decisions that outlast individual technologies — framework- and language-independent.
about.5: I help companies build architecture that works in practice. My working style is asynchronous and focused on what matters. My feedback is direct — I say clearly what I see.
blog.heading: On systems, decisions, and structure
blog.placeholder: Here I'll share what's on my mind — about systems, decisions, and the question of how what you build lasts long-term.
calltoaction.heading: Let's talk about your concern
footer.legal.heading: Legal
about.who.heading: Philipp Höllinger
about.who.1: Software architect from Salzburg. After my computer science degree, I worked as a developer and architect — in the automotive industry, the public sector, and software consulting. With each of these domains, it became clearer that the decisive levers lie in the architecture — in the decisions that determine how a system grows and adapts.
about.who.2: What drives me: an obsession for the details that shape the future of a system. Architectural decisions that outlast individual technologies — framework- and language-independent.
about.done.heading: What I've done
about.done.1: I designed and developed two systems from the ground up — architecture and code. For the Austrian government as part of an EU project, I built a system that provides administrative information across borders. Automated data processing, releasing resources that were previously tied up.
about.done.2: I strengthened the security architecture of a microservice-based e-commerce platform. I measurably improved system performance through targeted redesign of database queries and logging architectures.
about.stand.heading: What I stand for
about.stand.1: Architecture that works in practice — anchored in the pipeline, traceable in every decision.
about.stand.2: Deliberate decisions that apply automatically create space. Standards in the pipeline ensure reliable deployments. Knowledge that lives in the structure is preserved — regardless of who works on the system.
about.stand.3: That is the standard I bring to every project I support.
about.work.heading: How I work
about.work.1: My working style is asynchronous. Questions, findings, and open points I collect continuously and discuss them bundled in one focused conversation.
about.work.2: The code, the tests, the pipeline integration — everything is designed to be understood and extended independently.
about.work.3: My feedback is direct — I say clearly what I see.
services.diagnosis.introduction: You have a specific concern — and want clarity. I work through your system — architecture, code structures, infrastructure — until the full picture is clear. Backed by what is actually present. Afterwards, you know exactly where you stand and what makes sense next.
services.design.introduction: You know where your system needs to go — now it needs the architecture to get there. From module structure through quality rules to the roadmap — justified, documented, and immediately actionable. Afterwards, it's clear how your system is structured and which steps come next.
services.advisory.introduction: Decisions come up — technical, structural, directional. You want someone who knows your system and understands the full context. I review decisions, evaluate directions, and give clear feedback. Asynchronous, focused, at the right moment.
services.handson.introduction: You want architecture and implementation to emerge together. I work directly in the codebase — every feature, every module follows the architecture. In the Spring ecosystem, with Domain Driven Design and hexagonal architecture. What remains: the ArchUnit tests and the pipeline integration that anchor the architecture permanently.
faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Your question isn't here? Let's clarify it in the initial conversation.
faq.answer1: In the Hands-on service I work directly in the codebase — every feature follows the architecture. For Diagnosis, Design, and Advisory the focus is on analysis, architecture design, and guidance.
faq.question2: Do you recommend specific tools or technologies?
faq.answer2: No. I define the structural framework — framework- and language-independent. The choice of specific technologies is an independent decision within that framework.
faq.question3: Do you work within agile frameworks?
faq.answer3: No. Architecture work requires concentrated, continuous analysis. My working style is built for that — asynchronous, at my own pace, with the space that this kind of thinking demands.
faq.question4: Do you also do frontend implementation?
faq.answer4: My focus is on backend architecture — in the Spring ecosystem, with Domain Driven Design and hexagonal architecture. Frontend implementation is outside my scope.
faq.question5: Do you also do bugfixing or debugging?
faq.answer5: My focus is on architecture. If a bug points to a structural issue, that is part of the diagnosis — isolated bugfixing is not something I offer.
consent.message: This website uses cookies for usage analysis.
cookies.heading: Cookie Policy
cookies.what.heading: What are cookies
cookies.what: Cookies are small text files that are stored on the user's device when visiting a website. They serve to make websites more user-friendly and efficient. A distinction is made between first-party cookies, which are set by the website being visited, and third-party cookies, which are set by external services integrated into the website.
cookies.legal.heading: Legal basis
cookies.legal: The use of cookies is governed by §165(3) of the Austrian Telecommunications Act 2021 (TKG 2021), which implements Art. 5(3) of the ePrivacy Directive. Technically necessary cookies are set on the basis of the legitimate interest of the data controller pursuant to Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR, as they are required for the proper operation of the website. Non-essential cookies are only set with the explicit consent of the user pursuant to Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR.
cookies.types.heading: Types of cookies used
cookies.types.necessary.label: Necessary cookies
cookies.types.necessary: These cookies are required for the basic functionality and security of the website. They are set without consent. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR.
cookies.types.analytics.label: Analytics cookies (Google Analytics)
cookies.types.analytics: These cookies are used to measure website usage and to improve the website based on usage data. They are only set with the explicit consent of the user. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR. Provider: Google Ireland Limited, Gordon House, Barrow Street, Dublin 4, Ireland. Retention period: 14 months. Further details are provided in the privacy policy.
cookies.list.heading: Cookie list
cookies.list.name: Name
cookies.list.gastar.name: _ga_*
cookies.manage.heading: Managing cookies
cookies.manage: Cookies can be managed and deleted at any time via the browser settings. Consent to non-essential cookies can be withdrawn at any time. Instructions for managing cookies can be found in the help sections of the respective browser.
cookies.updates.heading: Updates to this policy
cookies.updates: This cookie policy may be updated to reflect changes in the cookies used or for legal reasons. The date of the last update is shown at the top of this page.
privacy.cookies.reference: Details on the cookies used on this website are provided in the cookie policy.
privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.phone.label: Phone
privacy.scope: This privacy policy serves to inform users of this website, in accordance with Art. 13 of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), about the nature, scope, and purpose of the collection and use of personal data by the data controller. The data controller takes the protection of personal data very seriously and treats such data confidentially and in accordance with applicable legal provisions.
privacy.controller.heading: Data controller
privacy.personal.heading: Processing of personal data
privacy.personal: Personal data is collected on this website only to the extent that is technically necessary. Processing is purpose-bound and data-minimized. Collected data is under no circumstances disclosed to third parties without a legal basis. The processing of personal data is based on the following legal grounds: Consent pursuant to Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR — the data subject has given consent to the processing of their personal data for one or more specific purposes. Performance of a contract pursuant to Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR — processing is necessary for the performance of a contract to which the data subject is party, or in order to take steps at the request of the data subject prior to entering into a contract. Legitimate interest pursuant to Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR — processing is necessary for the purposes of the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by a third party, except where such interests are overridden by the interests or fundamental rights and freedoms of the data subject.
privacy.rights.heading: Rights of data subjects
privacy.rights: As a data subject, you have the following rights: Right of access (Art. 15 GDPR) — You have the right to obtain confirmation as to whether personal data is being processed, as well as information about such data and its processing. Right to rectification (Art. 16 GDPR) — You have the right to request the rectification of inaccurate personal data. Right to erasure (Art. 17 GDPR) — You have the right to request the erasure of your personal data, provided the legal requirements are met. Right to restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR) — You have the right to request the restriction of processing of your personal data. Right to data portability (Art. 20 GDPR) — You have the right to receive your personal data in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format. Right to object (Art. 21 GDPR) — You have the right to object at any time, on grounds relating to your particular situation, to the processing of personal data concerning you.
privacy.withdrawal.heading: Right of withdrawal
privacy.withdrawal: Pursuant to Art. 7(3) GDPR, you have the right to withdraw any consent given at any time. The lawfulness of processing carried out on the basis of consent before its withdrawal is not affected. Withdrawal can be declared by email.
privacy.complaint.heading: Right to lodge a complaint
privacy.complaint: If you believe that the processing of your personal data violates the GDPR, you have the right, pursuant to Art. 77 GDPR, to lodge a complaint with the competent supervisory authority.
privacy.ssl.heading: SSL/TLS encryption
privacy.ssl: For security reasons and to protect the transmission of personal data, this website uses SSL or TLS encryption. An encrypted connection can be identified by the lock symbol in the browser bar and by the address bar beginning with "https://".
privacy.cookies.heading: Cookies
privacy.links.heading: Links to third-party websites
privacy.links: This website contains links to third-party websites. When accessing these external links, data may be transmitted to the respective operator. The data controller assumes no liability for the content and data processing by third parties. It is recommended to review the privacy policies of linked websites.
privacy.hosting.heading: Hosting
privacy.hosting: This website is hosted on GitHub Pages, a service provided by GitHub, Inc., 88 Colin P Kelly Jr St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA. When accessing this website, information is automatically recorded in so-called server log files, which the browser transmits automatically. This includes: IP address, date and time of access, requested resources, browser and operating system used. This data is stored temporarily for security purposes and deleted after a short period. Processing is based on the legitimate interest pursuant to Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR to ensure the uninterrupted operation of the website. For the transfer of personal data to the USA (third country), GitHub employs standard contractual clauses pursuant to Art. 46(2)(c) GDPR. GitHub is also certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.
privacy.analytics.heading: Google Analytics
privacy.analytics: This website uses Google Analytics, a web analytics service provided by Google Ireland Limited, Gordon House, Barrow Street, Dublin 4, Ireland. Google Analytics is activated exclusively with the consent of the user pursuant to Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR. Cookie consent is stored for 30 days. The following data is collected: pages visited and timestamps, interactions on the website, traffic source, device and browser information, geographic information (country/region), IP address (anonymized). Data is retained for 14 months. Google employs standard contractual clauses pursuant to Art. 46(2)(c) GDPR and is certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.
privacy.analytics.more: Further information on how Google Analytics handles user data can be found in Google's privacy policy.

services.process.heading: How it works
services.deliverables.heading: What you get
services.duration.label: Duration
services.investment.label: Investment
services.diagnosis.process.1.title: First call
services.diagnosis.process.1.description: We get to know each other and discuss your concern.
services.diagnosis.process.2.title: Scope & access
services.diagnosis.process.2.description: We define the scope. I get access to the relevant parts of the system.
services.diagnosis.process.3.title: Investigation
services.diagnosis.process.3.description: I investigate what we defined — layer by layer, until the full picture is clear.
services.diagnosis.process.4.title: Documentation
services.diagnosis.process.4.description: I document every insight: where it is, what causes it, and what impact it has.
services.diagnosis.process.5.title: Final walkthrough
services.diagnosis.process.5.description: We go through the results together. You get the documented analysis and the recommended next steps.
services.diagnosis.deliverable.2: Identified root causes with concrete evidence from your system
services.diagnosis.deliverable.3: Clear prioritization of results by urgency and impact
services.design.process.1.title: First call
services.design.process.1.description: We get to know each other and discuss what you need.
services.design.process.2.title: Scope & context
services.design.process.2.description: We define the scope. I understand your system, your constraints, and your goals.
services.design.process.3.title: Architecture design
services.design.process.3.description: I design the target architecture — how the system is built, where the boundaries are, and how the parts work together.
services.design.process.4.title: Rule set & Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
services.design.process.4.description: I define the quality and architecture rules and document every decision in an ADR. The rules are independent of specific tools and verifiable as automated tests in the pipeline.
services.design.process.5.title: Final walkthrough
services.design.process.5.description: We go through the architecture together. You get the module structure, the rule set, the ADRs, and a prioritized roadmap.
services.design.deliverable.1: Module structure — how a module is built
services.design.deliverable.2: Rule set — quality and architecture rules, independent of specific tools, implementable as automated tests
services.design.deliverable.3: Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) — every decision justified
services.design.deliverable.4: Transition strategy — module-by-module plan for existing systems
services.advisory.process.1.title: First call
services.advisory.process.1.description: We get to know each other and discuss what you need.
services.advisory.process.2.title: Onboarding
services.advisory.process.2.description: I learn your system, your constraints, and your goals. We define focus areas and cadence.
services.advisory.process.3.title: Ongoing guidance
services.advisory.process.3.description: I accompany ongoing architectural decisions with informed feedback.
services.advisory.process.4.title: Adaptation
services.advisory.process.4.description: The cadence adapts — it follows the decisions that come up.
services.advisory.deliverable.3: Ongoing structural guidance as your system evolves
services.handson.process.1.title: First call
services.handson.process.1.description: We get to know each other and discuss what you need.
services.handson.process.2.title: Scope & setup
services.handson.process.2.description: We define the architectural scope — modules, boundaries, and the rules that apply from that point on.
services.handson.process.3.title: Implementation
services.handson.process.3.description: I implement feature by feature — each follows the defined architecture with clean module boundaries.
services.handson.process.4.title: Anchoring
services.handson.process.4.description: Automated tests and pipeline integration ensure the architecture stands the test of time.
services.handson.process.5.title: Conclusion
services.handson.process.5.description: We go through the architecture together — the implementation, the tests, and the pipeline integration.
services.handson.deliverable.2: Modular structure with Domain Driven Design and hexagonal architecture
services.ideal.heading: Starting point
services.differentiator.heading: The standard
services.diagnosis.ideal.1: You want clarity about the architecture of your system.
services.diagnosis.ideal.2: You want to understand how the parts of your system interact.
services.diagnosis.ideal.3: You want a solid answer before you act.
services.diagnosis.differentiator: Every insight points to an exact location in your system. The path from concern to root cause is documented step by step and independently traceable. The results are immediately usable as a foundation for the next steps.
services.design.ideal.1: You have a clear vision of where your system needs to go.
services.design.ideal.2: You're facing a system overhaul, a modernization, or a new build.
services.design.ideal.3: You want a clear plan that can be executed immediately.
services.design.differentiator: Every decision is justified and documented. The specifications are programming language- and tool-agnostic — they apply regardless of the technology landscape. Every rule is defined to be automatically verifiable.
services.advisory.ideal.1: Your system is growing — you want to make sure the architecture grows with it.
services.advisory.ideal.2: You value informed feedback on structural decisions.
services.advisory.ideal.3: You want ongoing guidance — targeted and asynchronous.
services.advisory.differentiator: Every review considers the full architecture of your system. My feedback is independent and aligned with your specific concern.
services.advisory.scope.excluded.2: Attending daily standups or sprint meetings
services.handson.ideal.1: You want architecture and implementation to emerge together.
services.handson.ideal.2: You want someone who works in the Spring ecosystem and builds modular hexagonal architecture.
services.handson.ideal.3: You want structural decisions to be ensured automatically.
services.handson.differentiator: With every feature, architecture comes first — every module follows the same clear structure. Domain Driven Design with hexagonal architecture, secured by ArchUnit tests in the pipeline. Whoever reads the code, understands the architecture.
